One thing I have done in this course that has made me proud is staying consistent with my work and completing assignments on time. In the past, adding other responsibilities to my already-packed schedule would leave me feeling overwhelmed. This course helped me build better habits by planning ahead and staying organized, which made a noticeable difference in both my stress level and the quality of my work.

The biggest challenge I faced was the weekly discussions. Finding time to return to the discussion after I uploaded my post was difficult, and I felt rushed to add something relevant at the last minute. Moving forward, I plan to write my discussion replies and post them as I upload my post. By doing this, I will be able to cleanly wrap up the task and keep my weekly schedule on track, rather than circling back near the week's end and avoiding the feeling that everything is coming due all at once.

The skills I gained in this course, time management, setting SMART goals, organization, and self-reflection, will be increasingly helpful in future classes and in my career.

This being my first course with UAGC, I am proud of myself for completing assignments on time and working ahead. I knew this program would be more fast-paced, but I was able to keep up. In the past, I have gotten overwhelmed by the number of assignments, the readings, and the due dates. I found this class manageable with my schedule, and the module model was easy to understand. I am not sure if this model is used in other courses, but I found it easy to keep track and work ahead.

The biggest challenge I experienced in this class was the overcommunication with my advisor. This is not something I was used to, and the emails and calls are very frequent. However, I do now realize and appreciate the purpose of this. It makes me feel good knowing I have someone who checks in on me and is available for any questions I might have. This support is not something I felt with other universities I have been a part of.

One skill I learned was how to set goals properly. Using SMART goals is something I will continue to practice in both the academic and personal spaces.

Having a strong support network is a huge piece of having a positive and successful academic career. Being a working mom, I could not do this alone. I have a supportive partner who understands my need to complete assignments and study, and that I can't always be available. They work to keep our household afloat and to give me the space and time to accomplish what I need.

Week 5 - Quiz

• Due Feb 5 at 11:59pm

• Points10

• Questions10

• Time Limit None

• Allowed Attempts Unlimited

Instructions

SSC Exploration College/Career Reading Strategies

[WLO: 3] [CLOs: 1, 4]

Reading is a fundamental activity in academic, professional, and life pursuits. Reading needs to be approached with a growth mindset and a clear plan of best strategies to achieve understanding and actively engage with the material. Reading well and reading actively are key skills in school, work, and life. The quiz is worth 1% of your course grade.

| Prepare

Prior to taking this quiz:

• Read Chapter 8: Reading Strategies.

• Go to the SSC Active Reading page,

• Watch How to Read Your Textbooks More Efficiently

• Complete the learning activities on each of the three tabs (Active Reading, SQ3R Reading Strategies, and Note-taking).

The quiz contains 10 multiple-choice and true or false questions. You have unlimited time and unlimited attempts to take this quiz. To begin the quiz, select Take the Quiz. When finished, select Submit Quiz.

items.title: Week 5 - Final Project
items.plainText:
Personal Development Plan

[WLOs: 2, 4] [CLOs: 1, 2, 4]

For your final project, you have an exciting opportunity to apply what you’ve learned throughout this course in your own personal development plan. Why a personal development plan? Personal development expert Jim Rohn (2018) says, “When you look at successful people, you will almost always discover a plan behind their success. It is the foundation for success.” In other words, it is not effective to figure out how to reach your goals without a plan. You should view this personal development plan as the map you will need to reach your academic and professional goals.

This final project provides an opportunity to create a detailed plan for your academic and career-focused goals. In this personal development plan, you will include professional and academic SMART goals, reflect on the impact of mindfulness and motivation on achieving your goals, and reflect on the last five weeks in this course. You will upload this final project in your Folio, and it will be included as part of your earned points when it is graded in Waypoint. Remember, you learned how to upload documents into Folio in Week 2 Starter Kit and Learning Strategies and Tools quiz. Pay attention to the Folio instructions below and the grading rubric.

Prior to working on the final project:

Watch

• Personal Development Plan video

| Reflect

Reflect on what you have learned about yourself in this course.

• Read Chapter 12: From College to Career.

• Read Chapter 14: Professional Supports and Resources.

• Review the grading rubric for this assignment.

| Write

• Download the .

• Complete the six sections of the Personal Development Plan template.

• You will need to document your Live Learning attendance date. If you have been unable to attend Live Learning email your instructor for more information.

• Save the completed template to your computer.

Submit Your Assignment

• Upload your final project to Folio.

• Paste the link to your Folio project on the Personal Development Plan template.

• Submit your final project to Waypoint.

• Be sure to double-check that it was properly saved and successfully uploaded.

Need Help?

Explore the links for answers to these questions:

• Where can I find general writing tips? Visit the Writing Center.

• Can someone look over my paper before I submit it? Try Paper Review.

• Carefully review the Grading Rubric for the criteria that will be used to evaluate your assignment.

Waypoint Assignment Submission

The assignments in this course will be submitted to Waypoint. Please refer to the instructions below to submit your assignment.

• Click on the Assignment Submission button below. The Waypoint "Student Dashboard" will open in a new browser window.

• Browse for your assignment.

• Click Upload.

• Confirm that your assignment was successfully submitted by viewing the appropriate week's assignment tab in Waypoint.

For more detailed instructions, refer to the Waypoint Tutorial.
